{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mobile App Developer",
  "description": "Proforce Galaxies is a forward-looking advanced space technology and defense-focused company committed to delivering advanced satellite and space-based solutions that support national security, strategic intelligence, and critical infrastructure protection. Our capabilities are centered on the design, development, and deployment of high-performance systems that enable secure communications, real-time surveillance, and data-driven decision-making. At Proforce Galaxies, we are dedicated to strengthening technological sovereignty, enhancing situational awareness, and advancing aerospace innovation to support mission-critical operations and long-term national and global security objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mobile App Developer
Job Location:&nbsp;Ode-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview


	We are seeking a highly skilled and innovative Mobile App Developer to join our growing team of engineers and technology professionals.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing and building intuitive, scalable, and high-performance mobile applications that provide users with seamless access to real-time satellite data and AI-powered insights.


Key Responsibilities


	Mobile Application Development: Design, develop, and maintain cross-platform mobile applications (Android &amp; iOS) using React Native.
	API Integration: Collaborate with Software team to integrate RESTful APIs, cloud services, and real-time geospatial data.
	Data Visualization: Work closely with GIS analysts and AI engineers to develop interactive dashboards, maps, and visualization tools.
	Performance Optimization: Ensure applications are secure, responsive, and optimized for performance, scalability, and offline functionality where required.
	Cross-Platform Consistency: Deliver a consistent and high-quality user experience across multiple devices and platforms.
	Testing &amp; Debugging: Perform unit, integration, and functional testing to ensure application reliability and stability.
	Collaboration: Engage with cross-functional teams (frontend, backend, AI) to deliver integrated solutions.
	Continuous Improvement: Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in mobile development.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree (B.Sc.) in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in mobile application development or a similar role.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (mandatory requirement).
	Solid understanding of REST APIs, JSON, and third-party integrations.
	Experience deploying applications to the Google Play Store and Apple App Store.
	Familiarity with geospatial tools and mapping technologies (e.g., Mapbox, Leaflet, Google Maps API) is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of mobile security best practices and offline-first architecture.
	Experience with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is desirable.


What We Offer


	Salary:&nbsp;N250,000 &ndash; N350,000 monthly.
	Opportunity to contribute to Africa&rsquo;s rapidly evolving space-tech ecosystem.
	A dynamic, collaborative, and innovation-driven work environment.
	Exposure to high-impact, globally relevant projects.
	Continuous professional development and growth opportunities.
	Competitive compensation and benefits package.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV), Portfolio or links to completed projects and evidence of hands-on experience to:&nbsp;hrgalaxies@proforcedefence.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Mobile App Developer Job at Proforce Galaxies Limited




        
    

        
        Proforce Galaxies is a forward-looking advanced space technology and defense-focused company committed to delivering advanced satellite and space-based solutions that support national security, strategic intelligence, and critical infrastructure protection. Our capabilities are centered on the design, development, and deployment of high-performance systems that enable secure communications, real-time surveillance, and data-driven decision-making. At Proforce Galaxies, we are dedicated to strengthening technological sovereignty, enhancing situational awareness, and advancing aerospace innovation to support mission-critical operations and long-term national and global security objectives.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mobile App Developer
Job Location: Ode-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview


	We are seeking a highly skilled and innovative Mobile App Developer to join our growing team of engineers and technology professionals.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing and building intuitive, scalable, and high-performance mobile applications that provide users with seamless access to real-time satellite data and AI-powered insights.


Key Responsibilities


	Mobile Application Development: Design, develop, and maintain cross-platform mobile applications (Android & iOS) using React Native.
	API Integration: Collaborate with Software team to integrate RESTful APIs, cloud services, and real-time geospatial data.
	Data Visualization: Work closely with GIS analysts and AI engineers to develop interactive dashboards, maps, and visualization tools.
	Performance Optimization: Ensure applications are secure, responsive, and optimized for performance, scalability, and offline functionality where required.
	Cross-Platform Consistency: Deliver a consistent and high-quality user experience across multiple devices and platforms.
	Testing & Debugging: Perform unit, integration, and functional testing to ensure application reliability and stability.
	Collaboration: Engage with cross-functional teams (frontend, backend, AI) to deliver integrated solutions.
	Continuous Improvement: Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in mobile development.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s degree (B.Sc.) in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in mobile application development or a similar role.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (mandatory requirement).
	Solid understanding of REST APIs, JSON, and third-party integrations.
	Experience deploying applications to the Google Play Store and Apple App Store.
	Familiarity with geospatial tools and mapping technologies (e.g., Mapbox, Leaflet, Google Maps API) is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of mobile security best practices and offline-first architecture.
	Experience with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is desirable.


What We Offer


	Salary: N250,000 – N350,000 monthly.
	Opportunity to contribute to Africa’s rapidly evolving space-tech ecosystem.
	A dynamic, collaborative, and innovation-driven work environment.
	Exposure to high-impact, globally relevant projects.
	Continuous professional development and growth opportunities.
	Competitive compensation and benefits package.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV), Portfolio or links to completed projects and evidence of hands-on experience to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Network Administrator",
  "description": "Ekovolt&nbsp;is an enterprise technology services company empowering businesses online by providing affordable and reliable connectivity, together with local cloud and work productivity solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Network Administrator

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job Type:&nbsp;Part-time

Job Description


	We are seeking an experienced Network Administrator to join our team on a part-time, on-demand basis.
	This role is designed for a seasoned professional who can provide expert-level network support across multiple clients under our managed IT services framework, as well as support Ekovolt&#39;s own internet/ISP operations as needed.
	We are looking for a candidate with deep experience in wireless ISP networks, core network deployment, RF engineering, and client-facing technical support.
	The ideal candidate is self-sufficient, capable of managing complex network environments, and skilled at bridging technical gaps while working collaboratively with client internal teams.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide on-demand network administration and technical support, ensuring stability, performance, and security of their IT and network infrastructures.
	Manage WISP networks including point-to-point and point-to-multipoint links, to support client&rsquo;s internet operations and client environments.
	Configure, troubleshoot, and optimize network devices, with strong emphasis on MikroTik (RouterOS) and Cisco hardware.
	Manage end-user internet activations, troubleshoot customer connectivity issues, and ensure high-quality service delivery.
	Maintain accurate network diagrams, configurations, site documentation, and client reports.
	Provide orientation and technical guidance to client internal teams or technicians as needed, supporting their upskilling goals.


Qualifications


	5&ndash;7 years of experience in network administration, technical supervision, or operations, preferably within a WISP, ISP, or managed services environment.
	Proven experience with point-to-point and point-to-multipoint networks, RF analysis, and network cabling.
	Strong hands-on experience with MikroTik (MTCNA certification preferred) and Cisco devices.
	Solid understanding of routing, switching, VLANs, hotspot configuration, and customer activation processes.
	Excellent customer service and communication skills, with the ability to manage client relationships and expectations.
	Self-starter who can work independently, manage multiple client engagements, and respond to on-demand requests with minimal supervision.
	Must be available for on-site support in Lagos as needed and responsive during critical incidents.


Additional Information:

This role is ideal for an experienced network professional who values flexibility and variety. You will enjoy the autonomy of part-time, on-demand work while leveraging your full range of skills from wireless internet delivery and RF engineering to client engagement.",

Network Administrator Job at Ekovolt




        
    

        
        Ekovolt is an enterprise technology services company empowering businesses online by providing affordable and reliable connectivity, together with local cloud and work productivity solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Administrator

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Part-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced Network Administrator to join our team on a part-time, on-demand basis.
	This role is designed for a seasoned professional who can provide expert-level network support across multiple clients under our managed IT services framework, as well as support Ekovolt's own internet/ISP operations as needed.
	We are looking for a candidate with deep experience in wireless ISP networks, core network deployment, RF engineering, and client-facing technical support.
	The ideal candidate is self-sufficient, capable of managing complex network environments, and skilled at bridging technical gaps while working collaboratively with client internal teams.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide on-demand network administration and technical support, ensuring stability, performance, and security of their IT and network infrastructures.
	Manage WISP networks including point-to-point and point-to-multipoint links, to support client’s internet operations and client environments.
	Configure, troubleshoot, and optimize network devices, with strong emphasis on MikroTik (RouterOS) and Cisco hardware.
	Manage end-user internet activations, troubleshoot customer connectivity issues, and ensure high-quality service delivery.
	Maintain accurate network diagrams, configurations, site documentation, and client reports.
	Provide orientation and technical guidance to client internal teams or technicians as needed, supporting their upskilling goals.


Qualifications


	5–7 years of experience in network administration, technical supervision, or operations, preferably within a WISP, ISP, or managed services environment.
	Proven experience with point-to-point and point-to-multipoint networks, RF analysis, and network cabling.
	Strong hands-on experience with MikroTik (MTCNA certification preferred) and Cisco devices.
	Solid understanding of routing, switching, VLANs, hotspot configuration, and customer activation processes.
	Excellent customer service and communication skills, with the ability to manage client relationships and expectations.
	Self-starter who can work independently, manage multiple client engagements, and respond to on-demand requests with minimal supervision.
	Must be available for on-site support in Lagos as needed and responsive during critical incidents.


Additional Information:

This role is ideal for an experienced network professional who values flexibility and variety. You will enjoy the autonomy of part-time, on-demand work while leveraging your full range of skills from wireless internet delivery and RF engineering to client engagement.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at GE HealthCare
content:
GE HealthCare is a leading global medical technology and digital solutions innovator with over 100 years of healthcare industry experience and around 50,000 employees globally. We enable clinicians to make faster, more informed decisions through intelligent devices, data analytics, applications and services, supported by our Edison intelligence platform. We operate at the center of an ecosystem working toward precision health - digitizing healthcare, helping drive productivity and improving outcomes for patients, providers, health systems and researchers around the world.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Intern, Field Services - 12 Month Programme
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Nigeria
Sector: GE Healthcare
Duration: 12 Month Program

Job  Summary


	The Intern will be responsible for undergoing basic training on technical support on GE medical equipment, shadowing and observing exiting field engineers to understudy how equipment is serviced and managed.
	This will be a 12-month program.


Key Responsibilities / Essential Functions Include


	Technical: Will work and understudy experienced field engineers on various modality in the assigned area.
	Electronic/Mechanical: Basic level understanding about troubleshooting and technical safty (EHS)
	Customer satisfaction: Daily interface with doctors and technicians on equipment status issues. Acts to ensure satisfaction to primary customers. Assists in Account Sales visits; helps train others where necessary; identify Sales opportunities and communicate to account team.
	Productivity: Support any Warranty and PMS contract cost control.
	Administration: Responsibly completes all administration tasks on time.
	Installation: Plays a support role on basic installation process offsite. . Shares best practices. Maintain effective quality systems and programs compliant with ISO 9001 standard, according to the GE Service Quality Policy Statement.


Quality Specific Goals:


	Aware of and comply with the GEHC Quality Manual, Quality Management System, Quality Management Policy, Quality Goals, and applicable laws and regulations as they apply to this job type/position.
	Complete all planned Quality & Compliance training within the defined deadlines.
	Identify and report any and all customer quality or compliance concerns immediately to the Quality Organization.
	Identify and report any personal quality or compliance concerns immediately to the Quality Organization.
	Insure timely dispatch closure.
	Ensure completion of all field modifications instructions (FMI’s) within prescribed timeframe.
	Participate in continuous improvement activities by identifying and appropriately escalating process and product quality gaps, providing solutions when possible.


Qualifications / Requirements


	Qualified to National Diploma Two Holders (ND2) Cadre in selected Nigeria Ministry of Health certified Biomedical Schools.
	Technical analytical skills.
	Proficiency in English.
	Ability to work independently with minimum direction.
	High work standards and quality.
	Initiative and motivation.
	Plans and organizes work effectively.
	Excellent communications, listening and interpersonal skills.
	Strong Customer skills; deals tactfully and effectively with differences of opinion, influences rather than directs.


Required Qualification:


	Candidate must be eligible and currently due for their SIWES (Student Industrial Work Experience Scheme) placement.
	Applicants are required to attach an official SIWES placement/introductory letter from their institution as part of their application.
	Applications submitted without a valid SIWES letter will not be considered.


Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) Intern, Field Services (3 Month Program)

Job ID: R4038039
Job Location: Lagos
Sector: GE Healthcare
Duration: 3 Month Program

Job Description Summary


	The Intern will be responsible for undergoing basic training on technical support on GE medical equipment, shadowing and observing exiting field engineers to understudy how equipment is serviced and managed.
	This will be a 3-month program.


Key Responsibilities / Essential Functions


	Technical: Will work and understudy experienced field engineers on various modality in the assigned area.
	Electronic/Mechanical: Basic level understanding about troubleshooting and technical safty (EHS)
	Customer satisfaction: Daily interface with doctors and technicians on equipment status issues. Acts to ensure satisfaction to primary customers. Assists in Account Sales visits; helps train others where necessary; identify Sales opportunities and communicate to account team.
	Productivity: Support any Warranty and PMS contract cost control.
	Administration: Responsibly completes all administration tasks on time.
	Installation: Plays a support role on basic installation process offsite. . Shares best practices. Maintain effective quality systems and programs compliant with ISO 9001 standard, according to the GE Service Quality Policy Statement.


Quality Specific Goals:


	Aware of and comply with the GEHC Quality Manual, Quality Management System, Quality Management Policy, Quality Goals, and applicable laws and regulations as they apply to this job type/position.
	Complete all planned Quality & Compliance training within the defined deadlines. Identify and report any and all customer quality or compliance concerns immediately to the Quality Organization.
	Identify and report any personal quality or compliance concerns immediately to the Quality Organization.
	Insure timely dispatch closure.
	Ensure completion of all field modifications instructions (FMI’s) within prescribed timeframe.
	Participate in continuous improvement activities by identifying and appropriately escalating process and product quality gaps, providing solutions when possible.


Qualifications/Requirements


	Qualified to National Diploma One (ND1) Cadre or Higher National Diploma One (HND1) Cadre in selected Nigeria Ministry of Health certified Biomedical Schools
	Technical analytical skills.
	Proficiency in English.
	Ability to work independently with minimum direction.
	High work standards and quality.
	Initiative and motivation.
	Plans and organizes work effectively.
	Excellent communications, listening and interpersonal skills.
	Strong Customer skills; deals tactfully and effectively with differences of opinion, influences rather than directs.


Required Qualification


	Candidate must be eligible and currently due for their SIWES (Student Industrial Work Experience Scheme) placement.
	Applicants are required to attach an official SIWES placement/introductory letter from their institution as part of their application.
	Applications submitted without a valid SIWES letter will not be considered.


Click here to apply online

 

2.) General Manager - West Africa

Job Location: Nigeria

Essential Responsibilities


	Is accountable to achieve the quarterly and yearly Operating Plan for relevant Healthcare Portfolio in their geographical area
	Is accountable for timely and accurate forecasting of pipeline per product and sales per the normal reporting cycles within Geographical Zone.
	Provides input to and contributes to the formulation of the yearly business planning cycles
	Manages overall Sales revenue, margin, base costs and orders, and cash priorities in a manner that fulfils business goals and maintain focus on high impact.
	Actively identify & drive boundaryless selling opportunities. Promote Service offerings, identify and escalate commercial service needs.
	Execute Go-to-market strategy in conjunction with the relevant Modality leaders in order to cover the prioritized potential within the geography
	Align territories to market potential and priorities and assign optimal account management and modality specialist sales resources in conjunction with the modality teams.
	Develop & Implement growth strategies for the Private, Public & Regional business segments
	Attract, retain, educate and develop world-class commercial talent to execute on Go-to-market strategy.
	Responsible for ensuring that all Account Managers and Account Executives have clear opportunity management activity and relationship building plans to ensure efficient coverage of decision makers and influencers through-out the total opportunity management cycle.
	Drives performance management within the team, providing a regular operating mechanism of feedback, coaching and managing the annual appraisal system
	Know Key Opinion Leaders in care areas; manage professional relations with key customers, academia government & administrative bodies in order to deepen understanding of customer challenges and needs. Nurture relationship with professional society stakeholders within geographical area.
	Maintain up to date market and competitor knowledge related to the full Company product and services solutions and ensure that teams strengthen their knowledge on how to differentiate the Company offering.
	In cooperation with Product leadership and Marketing teams, drive One HealthCare marketing events in assigned region
	Understand and lead development of financial offerings and solutions in collaboration with sales and project finance team within LCT based on customer needs
	Ensure order booking quality and linearity improves for the zone, and ensure improved engagement/handshake between Inventory To Order (ITO) & Order To Remittance (OTR) takes place
	Ensure knowledge of and compliance of integrity and company policies, Systems, Quality Management Policy, Quality Goals, and applicable laws and regulations as they apply to this job type/position.


Qualifications/Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree.
	Experience in Business Management, Sales, Marketing or equivalent years of experience in progressive leadership positions within the Healthcare industry with in-depth knowledge of healthcare market.
	Proven and solid on ethics and integrity, able to guide the team and the organization safely in the demanding legal and regulatory HC environment.
	Experience closing large, complex, strategic deals.
	Experience in healthcare or equivalent industry.
	Strong business acumen with ability to analyze financial needs accurately and establish budget effectively.
	Proven leadership and ability to orchestrate resources and motivate teams. An inclusive leader who builds a connection to the workforce through personal involvement and trust.
	Proven ability to influence and drive change
	Proven success in the South Africa Public Sector
	Exceptional presentation skills; able to present ideas and communicate to customers and team in a way that produces understanding and impact.
	Understanding of customer/marketplace and drivers that influence customer behavior.
	Ability to resolve complex issues within LCT.
	Ability to develop and execute multiple priorities and approaches to meet objectives.


Preferred Qualifications


	Master’s degree preferred.
	Fluency in English language.
	Strong sales and management experience with significant track record.
	Strong management experience
	Must have valid authorization to work full time without any legal restrictions


Inclusion and Diversity:


	GE HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity Employer where inclusion matters. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.


Behaviors:


	We expect all employees to live and breathe our behaviors: to act with humility and build trust; lead with transparency; deliver with focus, and drive ownership – always with unyielding integrity.


Total Rewards


	Our total rewards are designed to unlock your ambition by giving you the boost and flexibility you need to turn your ideas into world-changing realities.
	Our salary and benefits are everything you’d expect from an organization with global strength and scale, and you’ll be surrounded by career opportunities in a culture that fosters care, collaboration and support.


Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "At Grado Konnects, we specialize in transforming ideas into actionable strategies that drive growth for individuals and businesses alike. Our unique approach ensures that you receive tailored guidance with a focus on professional development and success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Front Desk Officer is the first point of contact for visitors and clients.
	They are responsible for welcoming guests, handling inquiries, managing calls, and providing general administrative support to ensure smooth daily office operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome visitors in a professional and friendly manner
	Answer, screen, and direct phone calls and emails
	Receive, sort, and distribute mails and deliveries
	Maintain visitor records and issue access passes where necessary
	Schedule appointments and manage meeting room bookings
	Provide basic information about the company&rsquo;s services
	Keep the reception area clean, organized, and presentable
	Support administrative tasks such as filing, data entry, and documentation
	Liaise with staff and departments as required.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of an SSCE / OND / HND / B.Sc Degree in any related field
	Proven experience as a Front Desk Officer, Receptionist, or similar role is an advantage
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Neat appearance and professional attitude.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Customer service skills
	Time management
	Attention to detail
	Ability to work under minimal supervision
	Problem-solving skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;gradokonnects@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as subject of the mail.",

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Officer",
  "description": "BluePeak HR Services is a trusted Human Resources consulting firm delivering recruitment and personnel management solutionsto organizations across diverse industries in Nigeria. With manyyears of proven experience, we partner with businesses to build high-performing teams and implement sustainable HR practices that drive growth.

Headquartered in Port Harcourt, Rivers State, BluePeak HR Services provides nationwide support to companies seeking reliable and efficient human capital solutions. Our team of seasoned HR professionals combines local market insight with global best practices to help clients attract, develop, and retain top talent.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Procurement Officer will be responsible for sourcing, purchasing, and managing supplies required for smooth operation. The role involves ensuring the timely procurement of quality food ingredients, beverages, kitchen supplies, and operational materials while maintaining cost efficiency and compliance with company procurement policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Source and procure food ingredients, beverages, kitchen supplies, and other operational materials required for daily restaurant operations.
	Identify, evaluate, and maintain relationships with reliable suppliers and vendors to ensure consistent quality and competitive pricing.
	Prepare purchase orders, monitor deliveries, and ensure timely supply of materials to avoid operational disruptions.
	Conduct market research to obtain the best prices and negotiate favorable terms with suppliers.
	Monitor inventory levels and coordinate with the kitchen and bar teams to ensure adequate stock of required items.
	Inspect supplies upon delivery to ensure quality, quantity, and compliance with purchase specifications.
	Maintain accurate procurement records, supplier databases, and documentation for accountability and reporting.
	Track procurement expenses and support management in controlling operational costs.
	Ensure proper storage and handling of procured items in line with food safety and hygiene standards.
	Support management in implementing procurement policies, vendor evaluation, and cost-saving initiatives.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in a related field with a minimum of 2 years of experience in procurement, preferably within the hospitality or food service industry.
	Strong negotiation, vendor management, and cost control skills.
	Good knowledge of food ingredients, beverages, and restaurant supplies procurement.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic inventory management systems.
	Strong organizational, communication, and record-keeping skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resumes to:&nbsp;hr.bluepeak@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Procurement Officer Job at BluePeak HR Services




        
    

        
        BluePeak HR Services is a trusted Human Resources consulting firm delivering recruitment and personnel management solutionsto organizations across diverse industries in Nigeria. With manyyears of proven experience, we partner with businesses to build high-performing teams and implement sustainable HR practices that drive growth.

Headquartered in Port Harcourt, Rivers State, BluePeak HR Services provides nationwide support to companies seeking reliable and efficient human capital solutions. Our team of seasoned HR professionals combines local market insight with global best practices to help clients attract, develop, and retain top talent.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Procurement Officer will be responsible for sourcing, purchasing, and managing supplies required for smooth operation. The role involves ensuring the timely procurement of quality food ingredients, beverages, kitchen supplies, and operational materials while maintaining cost efficiency and compliance with company procurement policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Source and procure food ingredients, beverages, kitchen supplies, and other operational materials required for daily restaurant operations.
	Identify, evaluate, and maintain relationships with reliable suppliers and vendors to ensure consistent quality and competitive pricing.
	Prepare purchase orders, monitor deliveries, and ensure timely supply of materials to avoid operational disruptions.
	Conduct market research to obtain the best prices and negotiate favorable terms with suppliers.
	Monitor inventory levels and coordinate with the kitchen and bar teams to ensure adequate stock of required items.
	Inspect supplies upon delivery to ensure quality, quantity, and compliance with purchase specifications.
	Maintain accurate procurement records, supplier databases, and documentation for accountability and reporting.
	Track procurement expenses and support management in controlling operational costs.
	Ensure proper storage and handling of procured items in line with food safety and hygiene standards.
	Support management in implementing procurement policies, vendor evaluation, and cost-saving initiatives.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in a related field with a minimum of 2 years of experience in procurement, preferably within the hospitality or food service industry.
	Strong negotiation, vendor management, and cost control skills.
	Good knowledge of food ingredients, beverages, and restaurant supplies procurement.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic inventory management systems.
	Strong organizational, communication, and record-keeping skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resumes to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
